zh.Saint-Aubin-du-Pavail, Ille-et-Vilaine,: 法國布列塔尼易爾-吉維拉沙吉奧賓-度-帕瓦爾

zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Vis de Lou於大約1345年出生在法國時，她的父親Thomas vis de Lou47歲，母親Margaret Jernegan47歲。她於1374年與John Shelton Jernegan III在英國素福爾可瓦文利結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。她去世於1388年在英國素福爾可索莫利頓享年44歲，埋葬在英國素福爾可索莫利頓。

zh-hans.Saint-Aubin-du-Pavail, Ille-et-Vilaine,: 法国布列塔尼易爾-吉維拉沙吉奧賓-度-帕瓦爾

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Vis de Lou于大约1345年出生在法国时，她的父亲Thomas vis de Lou47岁，母亲Margaret Jernegan47岁。她于1374年与John Shelton Jernegan III在英国素福爾可瓦文利结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。她去世于1388年在英国素福爾可索莫利頓享年44岁，埋葬在英国素福爾可索莫利頓。

nameMeanings.name: Vis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Gerrit, Maarten, Teunis, Willem.
Dutch: from vis ‘fish’, a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman or fishmonger, or a nickname for someone thought to resemble a fish.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Lou
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ching-Yun, Jian, Wei, Chi, Hong, Jing, Jong, Kwan, Ting, Wai Keung, Zheng, Bin.  Vietnamese Son, Cao, Hao, Khanh Thi, Ly, Rung, Tien, Tin.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 婁, a name of one of the 28 Mansions in traditional Chinese astronomy: (i) from the second element of the placename Zhu Lou (邾婁), the name of a state (located in Zouxian in Shandong province) during the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771 BC ), also known as the state of Zou (鄒) or Zhu (邾). (ii) from the placename Lou (婁), the name of a fief (located in Zhucheng in Shandong province) in the state of Chu. (iii) from the Pi Lou (匹婁), Yi Lou (伊婁), Yi Na Lou (乙那婁) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in ancient northern China, who changed their original surnames to Lou (婁) during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ). (iv) said to be from the personal name of Li Lou (离婁), a legendary figure, famous for his good eyesight, during the reign of Huang Di, the ‘Yellow Emperor’ (c. 27th century BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樓, meaning ‘tower’ or ‘storey’ in Chinese: (i) from the title of Dong Lou Gong (東樓公, Duke Donglou of Qi), who was granted the state of Qi (杞) (originally located in Henan province, later moved to Shandong province) by King Wu of Zhou (c. 1087–1043 BC ). (ii) from the Gai Lou (蓋樓) and He Lou (賀樓) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who adopted the surname 樓 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ).

variants.name: Lu
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Wei, Ming, Jian, Min, Ping, Li, Yi, Feng, Ling, Hung, Minh, Nu, Gang, Ha, Muoi, Cheng, Chi, Hoa, Tan, Cuong, Ha To, Dung, Hai, Lan, Chung, Phong, Tuong, Tam, Chang, Manh, Shen, Tian, Tinh, You, Chong.

Chinese: Mandarin and Cantonese form of the surname 盧, meaning both ‘black’in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Lu (盧), the name of a fief (located in Changqing in Shandong province) granted to Xi, a great-grandson of Jiang Tai Gong or Jiang Ziya (c. 11th century BC ). (ii) shortened form of the compound Chinese surname Lu-Pu (盧蒲, also written as 蘆蒲), borne by descendants of the Duke Huan of Qi (ruler of the state of Qi, died 643 BC ). (iii) from the placename of Lu (廬), the name of an ancient state (unknown location) prior to the Xia dynasty (2070–1600 BC ). When it was adopted as a surname, the Chinese character 廬 was simplified to 盧. (iv) from the Mo Lu (莫蘆) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 蘆 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ), and later some descendants simplified the Chinese character 蘆 to 盧. (v) from the Tu Fu Lu (吐伏盧) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 盧 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534 AD ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 蘆, meaning ‘reed’ in Chinese: (i) shortened form of the compound Chinese surname Lu Pu (盧蒲, also written as 蘆蒲), which was borne by descendants of the Duke Huan of Qi (ruler of the state of Qi, died 643  BC ). (ii) from the Mo Lu (莫蘆) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 蘆 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Vis de Lou was born about 1345, in France, her father, Thomas vis de Lou, was 47 and her mother, Margaret Jernegan, was 47. She married John Shelton Jernegan III in 1374, in Waveney, Suffolk, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died in 1388, in Somerleyton, Suffolk, England, at the age of 44, and was buried in Somerleyton, Suffolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Vis de Lou est née environ 1345, à France, son père, Thomas vis de Lou, avait 47 ans et sa mère, Margaret Jernegan, avait 47 ans. Elle a épousé John Shelton Jernegan III en 1374, à Waveney, Suffolk, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée en 1388, à Somerleyton, Suffolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 44 ans, et a été enterrée à Somerleyton, Suffolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Vis de Lou um 1345 in Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Thomas vis de Lou 47 und ihre Mutter Margaret Jernegan 47. Sie hat 1374 in Waveney, Suffolk, England John Shelton Jernegan III geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1388 im Alter von 44 Jahren in Somerleyton, Suffolk, England und wurde in Somerleyton, Suffolk, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Vis de Lou è nata il circa 1345 nel Francia, sua padre, Thomas vis de Lou, aveva 47 anni e sua madre, Margaret Jernegan, aveva 47 anni. Lei sposato John Shelton Jernegan III nel 1374, nel Waveney, Suffolk, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì nel 1388, nel Somerleyton, Suffolk, England, all'età di 44 anni, e fu sepolta a Somerleyton, Suffolk, England.

ja.Saint-Aubin-du-Pavail, Ille-et-Vilaine,: フランスブレタグンイレ-エト-ヴィレアインサイント-アウビン-ドウ-プエイベイル
ja.Waveney, Suffolk, England: イギリススフォルクワベンエイ
ja.Somerleyton, Suffolk, England, United Ki: イギリススフォルクソメルリートン
ja.Great Yarmouth, Norfolk, England: イギリスノーフォルクグレアト ヤーモウス
ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Vis de Louが約1345年にフランスで生まれた時，父Thomas vis de Louは47歳，母Margaret Jerneganは47歳であった。1374年にイギリススフォルクワベンエイでJohn Shelton Jernegan IIIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼女は1388年にイギリススフォルクソメルリートンで44歳で死去し，イギリススフォルクソメルリートンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Vis de Lou(이)가 대략 1345년 프랑스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas vis de Lou(은)는 47세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Jernegan(은)는 47세였다. 그녀는 1374년 영국 수폴크 와븐이에서 John Shelton Jernegan III(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1388년 영국 수폴크 서머리턴에서 44세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 수폴크 서머리턴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Vis de Lou nasceu aproximadamente 1345, em França, seu pai, Thomas vis de Lou, tinha 47 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Jernegan, tinha 47 anos. Ela casou-se com John Shelton Jernegan III em 1374, em Waveney, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ela faleceu em 1388, em Somerleyton, Suffolk, Inglaterra, com 44 anos, e foi sepultada em Somerleyton, Suffolk, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Vis de Lou родилась около 1345 г. в местности Франция, её отцу по имени Thomas vis de Lou было 47 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Jernegan, 47. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Shelton Jernegan III в 1374 г. в местности вавини, суффолк, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Она умерла в 1388 г. в местности сомирлитон, суффолк, Англия в возрасте 44 лет и была похоронена в местности сомирлитон, суффолк, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret Vis de Lou nació aproximadamente 1345, en Francia, su papá, Thomas vis de Lou, tenía 47 años y su mamá, Margaret Jernegan, tenía 47 años. Se casó con John Shelton Jernegan III en 1374, en Waveney, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió en 1388, en Somerleyton, Suffolk, Inglaterra, a la edad de 44 años, y fue sepultada en Somerleyton, Suffolk, Inglaterra.
